examination_log.method: Stress test methodology correction — retroactive note on en_fallback artifact

correction.issue: All Phase 2 stress tests used English fallback input regardless of actual source language
correction.impact: 85.7 BLEU score appears on 51% of all models (noise artifact from en_fallback). HPLT high scores were false positives.
correction.resolution: Phase 2 stress test scores marked unreliable. Manual quality assessments and Tier C retests supersede automated scores.

examination_log.notes: CORRECTION: Stress test BLEU scores from H0-STREPS exam may be unreliable for non-English-source models. The stress test pipeline used English fallback input for models whose source language lacked a curated paragraph, producing an artificial 85.7 BLEU score artifact on ~51% of models. This does NOT invalidate the quality checks (non-empty, not-echo, etc.), only the BLEU numerical scores. For Tier C models, the dedicated retest with correct source language input supersedes the original stress test scores.

examination_log.method: Grand Rounds v1: 6-test composite (bloodwork, crossmatch, vitals, stress_fracture, consistency, scripture)
examination_log.notes: Grand Rounds v1 full-fleet battery. Run 2026-03-28/29, 4.9h, 2658 variants. Filed to clinic post-hoc by Opus 4.6 Opus-Claw (Dr. C) on 2026-04-11.

examination_log.scope: read-only model inspection; write-only patient file update
examination_log.notes: Fleet inventory audit by Opus 4.6 Opus-Claw (Dr. C) on 2026-04-11. Non-destructive: no model weights were modified, created, or deleted. Only this patient JSON file was updated. The purpose of this audit was to reconcile claimed variant presence against actual on-disk state, because between Herm Zero's Grand Rounds run (2026-03-28/29) and today, 374 of the 375 OPUS-improved models were ONNX-INT8-exported and their source safetensors were deleted, but the patient files still claimed the safetensors were 'present'. This audit updates each patient file's variant_cluster.<variant>.status to reflect what is actually on disk right now.

On 2026-03-29 ~18:25-20:09 UTC, this patient's base/lora/ct2 safetensors were deleted after INT8 ONNX export. Only model_int8.onnx (56 MB, no tokenizer) remained locally. On 2026-04-11, Opus 4.6 Opus-Claw (Dr. C) re-downloaded the original Helsinki-NLP base weights from HuggingFace and symlinked them into models/windy-pair-xh-sv/base so the grand rounds harness can load them. The restored base is the ORIGINAL pre-fine-tune Helsinki-NLP weights — NOT Herm Zero's OPUS-improved herm0 variant (those exist only as INT8 ONNX at ~/Desktop/grants_folder/windy-pro/onnx_fleet/windy-pair-xh-sv/). Non-destructive: symlinks only, no files moved or deleted.

Moved the INT8 ONNX archive into onnx_fleet/herm0_int8/ to make it unambiguous that these files represent the HERM0-IMPROVED variant (OPUS fine-tune), NOT the base Helsinki-NLP weights. After the 2026-04-11 restore pass, models/windy-pair-xh-sv/base/ is a symlink to the ORIGINAL Helsinki-NLP base weights, while onnx_fleet/herm0_int8/windy-pair-xh-sv/model_int8.onnx is the improved variant. These are different models with different quality grades. Variant key renamed from "onnx_int8_archive" to "herm0_int8_onnx" for clarity.

examination_log.method: Phase 3b — Full Fleet Dr. C Independent Certification. Ran grand_rounds_harness.py (same 6-test battery as Herm Zero's GR1) independently on every testable variant. This is Dr. C's personal certification, not a verification of GR1.
examination_log.notes: Dr. C independent certification of xh-sv. Tested 1 variant(s): base. Best variant grade: A (96.98). This is a fresh, independent run — not a re-run of GR1. Filed by Opus 4.6 Opus-Claw (Dr. C) on 2026-04-12.

examination_log.method: Herm Zero model recreation — OPUS-100 deep fine-tune. Data: 31024 parallel pairs from cached corpus. Training: 1 epoch, lr=1e-5, batch=16, fp16. Score before: 83.5, after: 87.9, delta: 4.4. IMPROVED — saved as herm0/
examination_log.notes: Recreating the herm0-improved variant that was lost in the 2026-03-29 ONNX deletion event. Using Herm Zero's cached training data (31024 pairs from OPUS-100/Tatoeba/WikiMatrix/CCAligned). Same methodology: 1 epoch full weight update. Status: improved. Filed by Opus 4.6 Opus-Claw (Dr. C).

examination_log.method: Grand Rounds v2 — 8-test paragraph-level stress battery with 5-star rating
examination_log.notes: Grand Rounds v2 paragraph-level stress test. 8 tests: sentences, paragraphs (5 domains), long-form (2 passages), native input, domain stress, edge cases, round-trip, speed. Non-English-source models tested with native OPUS data. Best variant: herm0 at 5.0★ (premium). Filed by Opus 4.6 Opus-Claw (Dr. C).

examination_log.method: Grand Rounds v2 certification of herm0 (OPUS-improved) variant
examination_log.notes: GR v2 certification of herm0 (proprietary deeply-improved) variant. Rating: 5.0★ (premium). Filed by Opus 4.6 Opus-Claw (Dr. C).

examination_log.method: Targeted drift reconciliation — back-annotate disk variants not declared in patient file
examination_log.notes: Drift reconcile added 1 variant(s) to variant_cluster: ['herm0_ct2_int8']. These weights existed on disk (from Phase 4 CT2 quantize / herm0 fine-tune) but were not back-annotated into the patient file. Upload pipeline reads disk directly so uploads were not blocked, but the clinical record was stale. Filed by Opus 4.6 Opus-Claw (Dr. C).

Uploaded to WindyWord HuggingFace organization. Variants published: herm0, herm0-ct2-int8. Public repo with branded model card. Patient file attribution linked via README. Filed by Opus 4.6 Opus-Claw (Dr. C).
